rationale: Gate-integrity attack on the adapter. (c) and (d) hold: flag-false leaves the slot missing so expected_reviewers>present trips gate rule (8) Block before threshold — fail-closed, verified; and panel create self-resolves reviewed_head_sha from --target (R2 passes no --sha, 110/ADR-0030), so a stale sha? arg cannot poison the staleness gate. But two integrity gaps remain. (a) The workflow interposes a Claude wrapper that transcribes codex stdout into the verdict file; unlike the skills path where codex writes its own file, this is a new non-deterministic step whose fidelity is unauditable — panel verify checks schema, not fidelity, and the spec never requires codex's raw stdout be persisted, so the 'plain reviewable files' control (§8) reviews the artifact against nothing. (b) R3's 're-prompt once or substitutes (R4) on a parse failure' conflates quota (codex rendered no verdict — substitution legit) with a REJECT-in-bad-JSON (codex DID render one). Re-prompt is not required to preserve the first verdict's semantics, and substitution would replace a rendered REJECT with a fresh claude-sub APPROVE — laundering a halt that gate rule (9) says must always block. A gate-integrity spec must forbid this in the Falsified clauses.
concrete_changes_required: R3 / Non-Goals: require the codex wrapper agent to persist codex's raw stdout as a tracked audit artifact (e.g. <slot>-round-<N>.codex-stdout.txt alongside the verdict, or embedded in the verdict), so the transcription from codex-output to verdict file is auditable — otherwise state explicitly that transcription fidelity is outside the trust model and why. As written, the wrapper indirection breaks the codex->artifact chain of custody the 'plain reviewable files' control (ADR-0037 §8) relies on.

R3/R4: separate parse-failure recovery from quota substitution. Specify that a parse-failure re-prompt MUST preserve the verdict semantics of codex's first response (fix only JSON formatting, never re-decide), and that if re-prompt still fails the slot fails CLOSED to a missing verdict (incomplete -> gate Blocks), NEVER a claude-sub substitution — because substituting a fresh reviewer for a rendered-but-malformed verdict launders it. Reserve R4 substitution for the quota case (codex produced no verdict). Add to R4's Falsified clause: 'falsified if a re-prompt or substitution can convert a codex REJECT/hard_block expressed in malformed JSON into an APPROVE, or discard a rendered verdict.'

R1 args / Requirements: state that the workflow's sha? arg is advisory-only and is never written to panel.json (create self-resolves from --target), or drop it — its consumption is currently unspecified.
findings: (a) Wrapper-agent transcription is a new fabrication/corruption surface absent on the skills path (where codex writes its own file). panel verify validates schema-conformance, not fidelity to codex's actual output; codex stdout is not persisted, so post-hoc auditability — the spec's implicit trust control — cannot detect a laundered verdict.

(b) HIGHEST severity: R3 'substitutes (R4) on a parse failure' conflates quota-wall substitution (legitimate — no verdict rendered) with parse-failure of a rendered verdict. A codex REJECT in malformed JSON could be re-decided on re-prompt or replaced by a claude-sub, destroying an evidence-bearing REJECT that gate rule (9) requires to halt regardless of vote count.

(c) VERIFIED STRENGTH: claude_sub_on_quota==false leaves the slot missing; expected_reviewers (create-stamped from mix) > present verdicts trips gate.go rule (8) incomplete->Block before the N-1 threshold rule (10) — fail-closed, correctly stated in R4.

(d) VERIFIED STRENGTH: R2's panel create invocation carries no --sha and 110 rev-parses --target itself (ADR-0030 executor boundary), so panel.json.reviewed_head_sha is create-captured; a stale sha? workflow arg is advisory and cannot corrupt the staleness gate (rule 6). Minor: sha? arg consumption is undocumented.
